channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What's next for Meta in the wake of trial losses and layoffs?
item.description: In the last few weeks, Meta has lost two lawsuits and seems to be scaling back the Metaverse. What's next for the tech giant?
item.content:encoded: In the last few weeks, Meta has lost two lawsuits and seems to be scaling back the Metaverse. What's next for the tech giant?
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Entertainment and California regulators push back against Warner-Paramount merger
item.description: California officials and Hollywood figures are pushing back on a proposed $111 billion Warner Bros.-Paramount merger, citing antitrust concerns, job risks, and media consolidation.
item.content:encoded: California officials and Hollywood figures are pushing back on a proposed $111 billion Warner Bros.-Paramount merger, citing antitrust concerns, job risks, and media consolidation.
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Trump administration sues three states over attempts to regulate prediction markets
item.description: The suits are the most ambitious effort to date that the Trump administration has gone to try to override state laws and set the rules for the fast-growing and increasingly divisive betting industry.
item.content:encoded:
The suits are the most ambitious effort to date that the Trump administration has gone to try to override state laws and set the rules for the fast-growing and increasingly divisive betting industry.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Have Trump's tariffs worked? This is where things stand a year after 'Liberation Day'
item.description: It has been a year since President Trump announced double-digit tariffs on imports from around the world. So far, those levies have not produced the economic boom the president promised.
item.content:encoded:
It has been a year since President Trump announced double-digit tariffs on imports from around the world. So far, those levies have not produced the economic boom the president promised.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: FIFA hikes World Cup prices, again. A finals ticket can now cost as much as $11,000
item.description: FIFA has raised prices for over 40 games of the World Cup in its latest sale that kicked off Wednesday, according to an NPR count. The most expensive tickets to the final now cost $10,900.
item.content:encoded:
FIFA has raised prices for over 40 games of the World Cup in its latest sale that kicked off Wednesday, according to an NPR count. The most expensive tickets to the final now cost $10,900.

(Image credit: Pool)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: The oil industry is betting big on plastics. Here's what that means for the future
item.description: Journalist Beth Gardiner says the fossil fuel industry is increasingly reliant upon plastic products. Her book is Plastic Inc.: The Secret History and Shocking Future of Big Oil's Biggest Bet.
item.content:encoded: Journalist Beth Gardiner says the fossil fuel industry is increasingly reliant upon plastic products. Her book is Plastic Inc.: The Secret History and Shocking Future of Big Oil's Biggest Bet.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Gas crosses $4 a gallon in the U.S. for the first time in 3 years
item.description: The war with Iran has driven up gas prices at a time when affordability is high on people's minds.
item.content:encoded:
The war with Iran has driven up gas prices at a time when affordability is high on people's minds.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Former Alex Jones employee says: 'It was nonsense, it was lies'
item.description: Josh Owens spent four years as a video editor and field producer for Jones' Infowars media company. "It was all about making things look cinematic," he says. Owens' memoir is The Madness of Believing.
item.content:encoded:
Josh Owens spent four years as a video editor and field producer for Jones' Infowars media company. "It was all about making things look cinematic," he says. Owens' memoir is The Madness of Believing.

(Image credit: Joe Buglewicz)
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: How Trump's EEOC is attacking DEI and emphasizing white people
item.description: Andrea Lucas, the Trump-appointed chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, has set a new agenda for an agency that long prioritized vulnerable and underserved workers.
item.content:encoded:
Andrea Lucas, the Trump-appointed chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, has set a new agenda for an agency that long prioritized vulnerable and underserved workers.

(Image credit: Elizabeth Gillis)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: The final batch of World Cup tickets is about to go on sale. Here are 5 things to know
item.description: FIFA is kicking off its last sales for World Cup tickets on Wednesday. From prices to why FOMO is working against you, here's what you need to know.
item.content:encoded:
FIFA is kicking off its last sales for World Cup tickets on Wednesday. From prices to why FOMO is working against you, here's what you need to know.

(Image credit: Robert Cianflone)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam
